oracle.established_by: Hakim's review of the identical commit (PR #792) caught this defect; the fix landed in the next review round.

ground_truth_defects.description: The prefix-strip loop in the pr-create gate consumes the real create verb, so a genuine `gh pr create` invocation can silently skip validation (a shape-4 gate-bypass, the same class of miss #47 closed for merge commands).

entries.notes: Canonical false-confidence case from spike #825 (row R1): a fluent, verification-heavy APPROVED review of a commit that carried a real BLOCKING defect Hakim caught on the identical commit. This is the case the prose-rubric method scored 15/16 — higher than 7 of the 10 genuinely-correct approvals in the spike's sample.

oracle.established_by: Both defects were fixed in the commit that followed this CHANGES REQUESTED review (6b19e2b5a); the fixes match exactly what was flagged.

ground_truth_defects.description: pr_base_repo scoped its primary gh query to the fork (head) repo as --repo, but a base-repo PR number can never resolve against the fork, so the query errors and falls back to the fork — the exact cross-fork divergence #765 set out to fix does not get fixed. All five marker writers still diverge from the base repo on a cross-fork PR.

test_pr_base_repo.sh's gh mock ignores --repo entirely, so the cross-fork test case passes even though the real gh call fails in that scenario — a false-positive test that certified a broken fix as working (9/9 green, 0/9 meaningful).

entries.notes: Rex correctly caught both the fork-scope regression and the vacuous test at this commit (spike #825 row R6, GOOD_CATCH). An earlier round on this same PR (commit f11415c41) had APPROVED — that miss is the human/Rex review the spike's row R2 refers to, but it isn't included as its own corpus entry here because the spike's own reviewer tag for that round is ambiguous.

oracle.established_by: Hakim's security review of the IDENTICAL commit (a99f1009d) flagged a HIGH finding the same day; Rex's code review of that same commit APPROVED. The forge-aware fix landed in the next commit (be732ce4c).

ground_truth_defects.description: The merge-gate hardening closed 'gh pr merge' and 'gh api .../pulls/<N>/merge' on GitHub, and 'glab mr merge' on GitLab, but left 'glab api .../merge_requests/<N>/merge' (the raw REST passthrough) ungated — the same #47 silent-bypass class, re-opened on the GitLab side.

entries.notes: Rex's code review of a99f1009d said APPROVED ('Merge-gate-critical...'); Hakim's security review of the SAME commit, same day, caught the HIGH finding Rex missed. A same-diff, cross-agent MISS — the cleanest possible pairing with hakim-767 below, since both entries score the identical commit through two different agents.

oracle.established_by: Both bugs were fixed in the commit that followed this CHANGES REQUESTED review (00f70aab2).

ground_truth_defects.description: A fix intended to close a structural-parse gap in the pr-create validator relied on a newline-collapse approach that is a no-op under bash 3.2 (macOS's shipped bash), so the gap it was meant to close stayed open on the platform most likely to hit it.
The regression test accompanying the fix didn't exercise the bash-3.2 code path, so it passed green while the fix it was meant to cover did nothing on that platform.

oracle.established_by: No later review or fix on this file ever contradicted the approval. The change was a one-line jq reserved-keyword rename (`def` binding) to fix jq-1.6 compatibility.

entries.notes: Spike #825 row R10 (GOOD_CLEAN) — a genuinely low-defect diff; a correct approval should score cleanly against an empty defect set.

oracle.established_by: No later review or fix on this file ever contradicted the approval. Docs-only change clarifying that the local marker, not GitHub's review state, is the merge-gate signal.

entries.notes: Spike #825 row R15 (GOOD_CLEAN) — the lowest-scoring correct approval under the disproven prose-rubric method, precisely because a docs-only diff gives a reviewer little to 'catch'. Included here deliberately: a good eval must not penalise a correct approval of a low-defect diff.
